desc: Ipoh is a heritage-rich food city framed by limestone hills, offering excellent value and easy rail access between Kuala Lumpur and Penang.
hls: Old-town murals, coffee shops, and colonial arcades
Limestone cave temples and karst scenery

todo.name: Ipoh Old Town mural trail
todo.desc: Walk the old quarter murals, lanes, and restored shophouses.

todo.name: Kek Lok Tong and Sam Poh Tong caves
todo.desc: Visit two limestone cave temples with gardens and chambers.

todo.name: Concubine Lane early visit
todo.desc: Arrive early for quieter photos and snack stalls.

todo.name: White coffee tasting route
todo.desc: Compare classic kopitiam and modern roaster takes on Ipoh white coffee.

todo.name: Perak Tong viewpoint climb
todo.desc: Climb the stair route for karst and city panoramas.

todo.name: Gua Tempurung half-day trip
todo.desc: Book a guided cave traverse outside the city core.

wifi.note: Good fiber in hotels and cafés; mobile data is strong citywide.

prac.fltNote: Ipoh has limited international service, so UK/Ireland visitors usually fly nonstop to Kuala Lumpur then continue by ETS train or road; school-holiday peaks (Jul-Aug, Dec) lift fares.
prac.currency: Malaysia local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Afternoon thunderstorms are frequent year-round and can flood low streets briefly.
Many popular eateries close once sold out by early afternoon; plan food stops early.
Ride-hailing is reliable, but cave-temple return pickups are slower outside central zones.

sweet: Jan-Mar and Jun-Aug for relatively drier sightseeing windows and easier cave visits.
